company_profile.name: AiRWA Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: AiRWA Inc. through its subsidiaries, operates artificial intelligence software that provides online matchmaking and dating experiences in Hong Kong, the United States, and the United Kingdom. The company was formerly known as Connexa Sports Technologies Inc. and changed its name to AiRWA Inc. in October 2025. AiRWA Inc. was founded in 2021 and is headquartered in Smyrna, Delaware.
company_profile.description_summary:
Operates artificial intelligence software that delivers online matchmaking and dating experiences across Hong Kong, the United States, and the United Kingdom. This platform generates revenue primarily through its dating and matchmaking services.

The company distinguishes itself by leveraging advanced artificial intelligence technology to power its matchmaking algorithms, differentiating its offering from traditional dating platforms.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

quality.note:
Moat score is null — AI Studio adds moat (0-10) and recalculates.
With moat: final_pct = (profitability + financial_strength + growth + predictability + moat) / 50 × 100.
Then re-classify using tiebreaker_inputs:
  ≥70%      → Safe (no penalty)
  65–69%    → Safe if 2+ of (roe≥0.13, roic≥0.13, eps_next_5y≥0.10) else Speculative (−3pts)
  55–64%    → Speculative (−3pts)
  50–54%    → Speculative if 2+ of (roe≥0.08, roic≥0.08, eps_next_5y≥0.08) else Dangerous (−8pts)
  <50%      → Dangerous (−8pts)
Moat override: if moat≥7 AND (profitability≥7 OR fs≥7) AND pct≥55 → Safe.
final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

quality_narrative.classification_reason: AiRWA Inc. carries a Dangerous rating because it shows significant weaknesses in profitability. Dangerous-rated stocks can still recover, but they carry a higher risk of permanent capital loss and should only be held with a clear thesis and strict position sizing.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: AiRWA Inc. demonstrates weak fundamental quality and is constrained by profitability, supported by a 17.37% ROIC. Key risks to watch: low consensus EPS growth (0.0% p.a.). The stock trades at a 57% discount to estimated intrinsic value and is warrants caution — careful position sizing and ongoing monitoring are essential.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: AiRWA Inc. demonstrates weak fundamental quality and is constrained by profitability, supported by a 17.37% ROIC.
quality_narrative.risk_profile: Key risks to watch: low consensus EPS growth (0.0% p.a.).
quality_narrative.investment_summary: The stock trades at a 57% discount to estimated intrinsic value and is warrants caution — careful position sizing and ongoing monitoring are essential.

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

forecast_meta.growth_explanation: Base (2.0%/yr): growth in line with analyst expectations. Bull (2.6%/yr): everything goes to plan. Bear (1.1%/yr): growth disappoints. All scenarios capped at 30% — higher rates produce unrealistically large numbers over time.

bull_points: Extreme valuation gap: stock trading at 57.2% discount to intrinsic value ($11.35 vs $26.48) combined with current price near 52-week low ($0.77), creating technical support and potential mean reversion catalyst over next 2-3 quarters.

Fortress balance sheet with 3.45x current ratio and only 0.19x debt-to-ebitda provides exceptional financial flexibility; $132M+ cash position with <$50M market cap gives management runway to execute AiRWA Exchange pivot without dilutive funding.

Recent insider accumulation signals management confidence: director bought $5.77M worth of shares in January 2026; continued insider buys despite 99% stock decline suggest conviction in crypto exchange thesis unfolding in H2 2026.
bear_points: Negative earnings trajectory: Q1 2026 revenue declined 8.33% YoY to $3M, with -3.5% net profit margin and 0% expected 5-year EPS growth; core licensing business lacks momentum to fund AiRWA Exchange development.

Massive institutional capitulation: Geode Capital exited 98.4%, UBS sold 99.6%, Vanguard dumped 94.6% of positions in Q3 2025, signaling fundamental concerns about business model viability; only Jane Street initiated new stake.

Regulatory and execution risk on crypto exchange: unproven RWA tokenization market faces SEC scrutiny; AiRWA Exchange timeline unclear with zero confirmed launch date; failed execution over next 6 months could trigger Nasdaq delisting warnings and accelerate stock collapse.

bull_points: Superior capital efficiency: ROIC of 17.37% significantly exceeds cost of capital, with strong 76.8% gross margins demonstrating pricing power and operational leverage; if core business scales, ROIC trajectory could fund growth without shareholder dilution.

Fortress balance sheet enables transformational strategy: 3.45x current ratio and 0.19x debt-to-ebitda provide balance sheet capacity for the $500M AiRWA Exchange JV; if RWA tokenization becomes $1T+ market over 5 years, early-mover advantage compounds valuation significantly.

Network effects and switching costs in matching platform: proprietary algorithms for 'Love and Marriage' matchmaking with 76.8% gross margins suggest defensible competitive moats; if crypto exchange drives user acquisition loop, technology licensing royalties could scale exponentially by 2028-2030.
bear_points: Structural profitability challenge: negative 3.5% net margin and zero growth guidance (0% EPS 5Y, 2.0% base growth) indicate core business cannot achieve sustainable profitability; dependence on unproven crypto exchange for valuation recovery creates binary outcome risk.

Severe shareholder dilution eroding per-share value: reverse splits (1-for-50, then 1-for-40) plus massive capital raises at depressed prices have diluted shares >5,455% YoY; even if enterprise value grows, per-share economics may not recover within 5-year horizon.

Unproven business model pivot with execution risk: company transformed from sports equipment to AI matchmaking to crypto exchange in <18 months; lack of track record scaling either matchmaking or crypto operations creates high probability of strategic failure and value destruction by 2028.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or significantly overvalued — better opportunities elsewhere.

company_profile.name: Yalla Group Limited
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Yalla Group Limited, together with its subsidiaries, operates a social networking and gaming platform in the Middle East and North Africa region. It offers mobile applications, including Yalla, a voice-centric group chat platform; and Yalla Ludo, a casual gaming application. The company's platform provides group chatting and games services; and consume virtual currencies to purchase virtual items, as well as provides upgrade services. It has operations in United Arab Emirates, Mainland China, Hongkong, Cayman Islands, Saudia Arabia, and Singapore countries. The company was formerly known as FYXTech Corporation. Yalla Group Limited was founded in 2016 and is headquartered in Dubai, the United Arab Emirates.
company_profile.description_summary:
Operates a social networking and gaming platform across the Middle East and North Africa. Its main applications are Yalla, a voice-centric group chat platform, and Yalla Ludo, a casual gaming application. Revenue derives from virtual currency sales for in-app purchases and upgrade services.

The platform uniquely emphasizes voice-centric communication, differentiating it from text-based competitors in its regional market.

<cite index="6-6,6-7">Average MAUs grew 7.7% year-over-year to 48.0 million in Q1 2026, with gaming business reinforced as key growth engine from new mid-core and hard-core games</cite>, positioning for user monetization acceleration.
<cite index="5-1">Q4 2025 net margin expanded to 41.2% on $83.9M revenue, demonstrating cost discipline despite higher S&M investments</cite>, providing cushion for near-term earnings surprises.
bear_points: <cite index="6-1,6-4">Q1 2026 revenues declined 5.8% to $79.0M from $83.9M in Q1 2025, primarily due to decreased paying users amid regional geopolitical challenges and Ramadan impact</cite>, signaling continued user monetization headwinds.
<cite index="6-12">Paying users declined to 10.5 million from 11.8 million year-over-year</cite>, undermining the utility of growing MAU metrics and raising concerns about ARPU sustainability.

<cite index="7-20">New game titles face monetization challenges, with revenue contributions expected only in second half of 2026</cite>, delaying near-term earnings accretion and creating execution risk.

bull_points: <cite index="41.69%">Exceptional ROIC of 41.69% demonstrates superior capital efficiency and durable competitive moat in game and social platform development</cite>, supporting premium valuation and long-term value creation.

<cite index="5-11,6-11">Strong balance sheet with $806.7M in cash as of March 2026 (9.02x current ratio) provides substantial capacity for aggressive R&D investments, M&A, or accelerated shareholder returns</cite> without financial constraint.

<cite index="3-13">Middle East gaming market projected to reach $7.1 billion in 2025 with 75% year-over-year growth, providing multi-year runway for Yalla's new game portfolio expansion and international scaling</cite>.
bear_points: <cite index="-5.8%">Current revenue declining 5.8% year-over-year with core legacy business expected to decline low to mid-single digits through 2026, indicating structural headwind rather than cyclical weakness</cite>.

<cite index="4">EPS growth constrained to 2.0% base rate (from 0.0% long-term) due to financial strength score of only 6/10, suggesting balance sheet or operational leverage limitations will cap shareholder returns</cite>.

<cite index="6-14,6-15">Company guidance projects full-year 2026 revenues broadly flat versus 2025, with materialization of double-digit growth contingent on new games scaling in 2027—creating execution risk and prolonged growth uncertainty</cite>.

company_profile.name: Yelp Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 19.2%; DCF methods prioritised
company_profile.description: Yelp Inc. operates a platform that connects consumers with local businesses in the United States and internationally. Its platform covers various categories, including restaurants, shopping, beauty and fitness, health, and other categories, as well as home, local, auto, professional, pets, events, real estate, and financial services. It provides free and paid advertising products to businesses, which include cost-per-click advertising and multi-location Ad products, RepairPal network, and business listing pages. The company also offers Yelp Connect that provides advertisers with a channel to market new offerings, such as new menu items and specials, and communicate business updates to customers; Yelp Guaranteed that provides limited coverage to consumers who hire a yelp guaranteed business through request-a-quote in the event; Nearby Jobs solutions; Yelp Guest Manager, a subscription-based suite of front-of-house management tools for restaurants, nightlife, and certain other venues, which include online reservations, a waitlist management solution, as well as through hostless kiosks, and seating and server rotation management tools; Yelp Fusion Insights program that offers business owners local analytics and insights; and Yelp Fusion, which offers free access to basic information through publicly available APIs and paid access to content and data for consumer-facing enterprise use. In addition, it provides content licensing and consumer-interactive tools. Further, the company offers its products directly through its sales force; indirectly through partners; and online through its website and business app, as well as non-advertising partner arrangements. It has partnership with DOORDASH to provide consumers with a service to place food orders for pickup and delivery. The company was formerly known as Yelp! Inc. and changed its name to Yelp Inc. in November 2005. Yelp Inc. was incorporated in 2004 and is based in San Francisco, California.
company_profile.description_summary:
Yelp operates a digital platform connecting consumers with local businesses across restaurants, shopping, health, home services, and other categories. It generates revenue primarily through paid advertising products for businesses, including cost-per-click ads, listing pages, and subscription tools like Guest Manager for restaurant management.

The platform differentiates itself through specialized offerings such as RepairPal network services, Yelp Guaranteed consumer protections, job listings, business analytics, and API access via Yelp Fusion. It also partners with DoorDash to enable food ordering directly through its platform.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 19.2%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

company_profile.name: Yext, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: Yext, Inc. provides a platform that offers answers to consumer questions in North America and internationally. The company operates Yext Digital Presence platform, a cloud-based platform that allows its customers to provide answers to consumer questions, to control the information about their businesses and the content of their landing pages, and to manage their consumer reviews, as well as to offer customers the ability to update their information and content through its publisher network of maps, apps, search engines, GPS systems, digital assistants, vertical directories, and social networks. Its platform also enables its customers to centralize, control and manage data fields, including store information comprising name, address, phone number, and holiday hours; professional information, such as headshot, specialties, and education; job information consisting of title and description; FAQs; and other information. In addition, the company offers professional services to customize the Yext platform, such as custom building landing pages and data integrations, as well as maintenance and other services; and a digital client engagement platform for financial services. The company serves various industries, such as healthcare, hospitality, food services, retail, and financial services. Yext, Inc. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in New York, New York.
company_profile.description_summary:
Yext provides a cloud-based digital presence platform that helps businesses manage their information across maps, search engines, apps, and social networks. The platform enables companies to control business data, manage consumer reviews, and answer customer questions across multiple channels. Primary revenue comes from subscription fees for platform access.

The company distinguishes itself by offering a centralized system for managing dispersed business information and a publisher network spanning diverse digital channels. It additionally provides professional services including custom landing pages and data integrations, plus a specialized engagement platform for financial services.

bull_points: <cite index="4-1">Q2 FY2026 revenue of $113.1 million represents 16% year-over-year growth</cite>, demonstrating near-term momentum and beat of company guidance.

<cite index="4-7,4-22">Company swung from net loss of $4.1 million in Q2 FY2025 to net income of $26.8 million in Q2 FY2026</cite>, showing sharp profitability inflection that should support earnings delivery in next quarter.

<cite index="5-8">Yext launched Scout MCP and Scout API, opening its visibility infrastructure to global partners</cite>, positioning the company as a data platform and unlocking new revenue channels for the next 3-6 months.
bear_points: <cite index="4-9">CEO Michael Walrath submitted a non-binding proposal on August 18, 2025 to acquire all outstanding shares at $9.00 per share in cash</cite>, creating uncertainty around future strategy and potential delisting risk that could depress near-term trading.

<cite index="4-4,4-19">Gross margin declined from 77.2% in Q2 FY2025 to 75.2% in Q2 FY2026</cite>, suggesting cost pressures that could squeeze profitability in the next earnings cycle if pricing or volume growth doesn't accelerate.
<cite index="6-11">Management expressed cautious outlook due to macroeconomic uncertainties</cite>, signaling potential revenue headwinds over the next 3-6 months despite near-term beats.

bull_points: <cite index="9-1">Yext moved from a $27.9 million net loss in fiscal 2025 to a $37.9 million net profit in fiscal 2026</cite>, validating the company's shift to profitable growth and demonstrating sustainable business model improvement aligned with 24.23% ROE.

<cite index="9-8">Dollar-based net retention of 99% for customers with $50,000+ ARR supports the strategy of shifting mix toward higher-value accounts</cite>, providing durable revenue growth and pricing power over a 1-5 year horizon.

<cite index="9-7">Adjusted EBITDA expanded from $67.0 million to $107.3 million with a 24% margin, reflecting meaningful operating efficiency gains</cite>, demonstrating operating leverage and the ability to scale profitably as the business matures.
bear_points: With debt-to-EBITDA of 7.35x, Yext carries substantial financial leverage that constrains its ability to invest in growth or absorb downturns, limiting the company's financial flexibility over a multi-year period.

Revenue growth of -1.0% combined with a low financial strength score of 5/10 has forced downward revision of EPS growth expectations from 4.8% to 3.6%, signaling structural headwinds that may persist across the 1-5 year horizon.

<cite index="4-1">14% constant currency revenue growth masks underlying softness, and with the current ratio of 1.07</cite>, tight liquidity leaves limited room for operational missteps or macro deterioration over the long term.

watchlist_signal.reason: Good fundamentals with a 24% margin of safety — worth adding at current prices.

company_profile.name: Yiren Digital Ltd.
company_profile.type_rationale: Consumer finance / payments — DNI and P/B prioritised; standard debt ratios unsuitable due to balance sheet structure
company_profile.description: Yiren Digital Ltd. provides financial services through an AI-powered Fintech platform in China. Its platform provides a suite of financial and lifestyle services. The company offers credit solutions, insurance brokerage, and others. The company was formerly known as Yirendai Ltd. and changed its name to Yiren Digital Ltd. in September 2019. The company was founded in 2012 and is based in Beijing, the People's Republic of China. Yiren Digital Ltd. operates as a subsidiary of CreditEase Holdings (Cayman) Limited.
company_profile.description_summary:
Operates an AI-powered fintech platform in China offering credit solutions, insurance brokerage, and lifestyle services. Generates revenue primarily through its financial services offerings delivered via this digital platform.

Distinctive for its integration of artificial intelligence technology into financial services operations and its positioning as a subsidiary of CreditEase Holdings, a major financial services group.

_meta.type_rationale: Consumer finance / payments — DNI and P/B prioritised; standard debt ratios unsuitable due to balance sheet structure
_meta.method_explanation: Consumer finance companies — such as credit card providers or personal lenders — earn income from the interest they charge on loans. Like banks, they carry a lot of debt by design because lending money is their core business model. We use net income DCF and Price-to-Book, which together capture both the earning power and the asset value of the loan book.

quality.company_type_note: Banks and REITs carry structural leverage — do not describe financial_strength scores of 7+ as 'moderate concern' for these types.
quality.note:

With moat: final_pct = (profitability + financial_strength + growth + predictability + moat) / 50 × 100.

  ≥70%      → Safe (no penalty)

  55–64%    → Speculative (−3pts)
  50–54%    → Speculative if 2+ of (roe≥0.08, roic≥0.08, eps_next_5y≥0.08) else Dangerous (−8pts)
  <50%      → Dangerous (−8pts)

final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
_notes.shares_vs_buybacks: When a company buys back its own shares, it reduces the total number of shares in circulation — which means each remaining share represents a larger slice of the business. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

bull_points: <cite index="4-5">Q3 2025 revenue grew 5.1% year-over-year to RMB 1.555 billion with financial services revenue rising 70%, demonstrating operational resilience and a return to profitability growth.</cite>

<cite index="4-7">Loan facilitation volume reached RMB 20.2 billion in Q3 2025, up 51% year-over-year, with average new loan size increasing 44%, signaling strong pricing power and market share gains.</cite>
<cite index="4-2">Indonesian operations launched in September 2025 with overseas revenue expected to exceed 10% in 2026, opening a new international growth vector ahead of Q4 2025 earnings.</cite>
bear_points: <cite index="4-6">Q3 2025 net income declined 12% sequentially to RMB 318 million and fell from RMB 355.4 million year-over-year due to higher provisions and margin pressure.</cite>

<cite index="3-4">Delinquency rates deteriorated sequentially in Q3 2025, with 1-30 day past-due loans rising to 2.7% from 1.7% in Q2 2025, signaling credit quality headwinds ahead of the next earnings cycle.</cite>

<cite index="1-3">Adjusted EBITDA swung to a loss of RMB 109.6 million in 2025 from a gain of RMB 1,776.2 million in 2024, reflecting severe margin compression under new regulatory constraints.</cite>

bull_points: <cite index="4-4">The company maintains a robust balance sheet with ample cash position, supporting ongoing fintech innovation and insurance business turnaround, with a current ratio of 6.82 and minimal debt-to-EBITDA of 0.02.</cite>

<cite index="4-3">Proprietary AI platform and automation enhance productivity and service quality while reducing labor costs, providing competitive moat and improving efficiency that could offset revenue headwinds.</cite>

<cite index="4-10">Next-generation fintech platform based on AI and blockchain launching in H1 2026, with International and Internet Insurance segments expected to drive higher revenue and margin growth over a 1-5 year horizon.</cite>
bear_points: <cite index="1-4,1-3">Earnings have collapsed with 2025 EPS of RMB 0.4670 (US$0.0668) versus RMB 18.2654 in 2024, and adjusted EBITDA swung to a RMB 109.6 million loss, reflecting structural profitability challenges that constrain the growth rate to 2% despite financial strength.</cite>

<cite index="1-5">Operating cash flow declined 52% to RMB 686.7 million in 2025 from RMB 1,424.1 million in 2024, signaling potential cash generation sustainability issues for long-term dividend and investment capacity.</cite>

The 34.1% revenue decline and minimal 1.0% profit margin leave limited financial flexibility for innovation investments or competitive battles, particularly as regulatory constraints in the fintech lending space remain elevated and unpredictable.

watchlist_signal.reason: Weak fundamentals or significantly overvalued — better opportunities elsewhere.

company_profile.name: Clear Secure, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 36.2%; DCF methods prioritised
company_profile.description: Clear Secure, Inc. operates a secure identity platform under the CLEAR brand name primarily in the United States. It provides secure identity platform, a multi-layered infrastructure consisting of front-end, including enrollment, verification, and linking, as well as back-end. The company also offers CLEAR Plus, a consumer aviation subscription service, which enables access to predictable entry lanes in airport security checkpoints, as well as access to broader network; and CLEAR mobile app, which is used to enroll new members and improve the experience for existing members. In addition, it provides RESERVE powered by CLEAR to reservation spot for airport locations; Home to Gate, a feature to help travelers plan and time their trip to the airport; CLEAR1, a B2B offering that extends secure identity platform to partners to create friction-free experiences for their customers; CLEAR ID, a free and mobile digital ID that allow any traveler to verify their identity; CLEAR Concierge which enhances the CLEAR value proposition for high-frequency and premium travelers, as well as travelers requiring additional assistance, such as families and senior citizens; CLEAR Perks, a suite of added benefits that help CLEAR members get an even more seamless experience; TSA PreCheck Enrollment Provided by CLEAR, as well as online renewal services; Sora ID, a one-click know your customer that enables adding know your customer services to platform offerings; and a virtual queuing technology. The company has a strategic collaboration with Samsung Electronics America, Inc. for the development of Samsung ID with CLEAR, a digital ID solution that allows users to add a US passport to Samsung Wallet and verify their identity. The company was founded in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York. Clear Secure, Inc. is a subsidiary of Alclear Investments, Llc.
company_profile.description_summary:
Operates a secure identity platform providing biometric and digital identity verification services. Primary revenue comes from CLEAR Plus, a consumer aviation subscription offering expedited airport security screening, supplemented by B2B identity solutions, TSA PreCheck enrollment, and ancillary travel services through its mobile app ecosystem.

Distinctive through strategic partnerships, including Samsung Electronics for digital wallet integration, and comprehensive service expansion beyond aviation into broader identity verification and frictionless customer experience platforms for enterprise clients.

_meta.type_rationale: FCF compounder — FCF margin 36.2%; DCF methods prioritised
_meta.method_explanation: Free cash flow compounders are businesses that consistently generate more cash than they need to run the business — think of companies like consumer goods brands or software firms with subscription revenue. Because their cash flows are reliable year after year, a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model works very well. DCF asks: if I add up all the future cash flows this company will generate and adjust for the time value of money, what is it worth today? For these companies, that calculation gives a meaningful and trustworthy answer.

company_profile.name: York Space Systems, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: York Space Systems, Inc. operates as a space and defense prime providing a comprehensive suite of mission-critical solutions for national security, government and commercial customers in the United States. It provides space and defense primes with proprietary hardware and software capabilities designed to address customers' requirements across the elements of the space ecosystem throughout the mission lifecycle. The company also engages in the design, production, integration, and operation of spacecraft to manage spacecraft and constellations. It offers S-CLASS, LX-CLASS, and M-CLASS spacecraft, which are satellite platforms to a range of space market needs. In addition, the company provides software that enables versatile integration of a variety of payloads for customers and supply chain commonalities across platforms, as well as software-enabled services. The company was formerly known as Yellowstone Midco Holdings II, LLC and changed its name to York Space Systems, Inc. in January 2026. York Space Systems, Inc. was founded in 2012 and is based in Greenwood Village, Colorado.
company_profile.description_summary:
Operates as a space and defense contractor providing spacecraft, satellite platforms, and integrated solutions for national security, government, and commercial customers. Designs, produces, and operates satellites across multiple classes while offering proprietary hardware, software, and payload integration capabilities throughout the mission lifecycle.

The company distinguishes itself through software-enabled services and standardized platforms that enable supply chain commonalities across its spacecraft offerings, allowing flexible payload integration for diverse customer requirements.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

company_profile.description: Yum China Holdings, Inc. owns, operates, and franchises restaurants in the People's Republic of China. The company operates KFC, Pizza Hut, and All Other segments. This chart shows how the share count has changed over time. Consistent buybacks are generally a sign that management believes the stock is undervalued and wants to return cash to shareholders.
_notes.returns_trajectory: Return on Equity (ROE) measures how much profit a company generates for every dollar of shareholder money invested. Return on Invested Capital (ROIC) is similar but also includes debt. Both are expressed as a percentage — higher is better. A company consistently earning above 15% ROIC is generally considered to have a genuine competitive advantage over its peers.
_notes.revenue_vs_ar: Accounts receivable is money the company is owed by customers who have not yet paid their bills. This chart compares revenue growth to accounts receivable growth each year. If accounts receivable is growing much faster than revenue, it may mean the company is struggling to collect payments — sometimes a sign of aggressive or fictitious revenue reporting. A healthy company should see both lines move at a similar pace.

company_profile.name: cbdMD, Inc.
company_profile.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
company_profile.description: cbdMD, Inc. produces and distributes various cannabidiol (CBD) products in the United States and internationally. The company owns and operates various consumer hemp-based CBD brands. Its cbdMD brand products include oils, gummies, capsules, soft-gels, topicals, functional formulations, and hemp-derived compliant Delta-9 THC products. It also provides products for dogs and cats, including tinctures, chews, and topicals under the Paw CBD brand name; non-cannabinoid functional ingredients, such as functional mushrooms under the ATRx brand; and hemp-derived solutions under the Herbal Oasis brand. It serves retailers, and health and pet channels through the direct-to-consumer channel, e-commerce platforms, online marketplaces, and wholesale distribution. The company was formerly known as Level Brands, Inc. and changed its name to cbdMD, Inc. in May 2019. cbdMD, Inc. is based in Charlotte, North Carolina.
company_profile.description_summary:
This company produces and distributes cannabidiol (CBD) products across the United States and internationally. Revenue comes from multiple consumer hemp-based CBD brands sold through direct-to-consumer channels, e-commerce, and wholesale distribution to retailers and health outlets.

The company operates distinct brands targeting different markets: cbdMD offers oils, gummies, and topicals for consumers; Paw CBD specializes in pet products; ATRx provides functional mushroom ingredients; and Herbal Oasis offers hemp-derived solutions.

_meta.type_rationale: Standard company — balanced weighting across all methods
_meta.method_explanation: This company does not fit neatly into a specialist category, so we apply a balanced mix of all ten valuation methods. These include Discounted Cash Flow (which estimates the present value of future cash flows), earnings multiples (P/E and PEG), revenue multiples (P/S and PSG), book value (P/B), and EV/EBITDA. Each method approaches value from a different angle — using all of them together gives a more rounded estimate than relying on any single number.

quality.note:
Moat score is null — AI Studio adds moat (0-10) and recalculates.
With moat: final_pct = (profitability + financial_strength + growth + predictability + moat) / 50 × 100.
Then re-classify using tiebreaker_inputs:
  ≥70%      → Safe (no penalty)
  65–69%    → Safe if 2+ of (roe≥0.13, roic≥0.13, eps_next_5y≥0.10) else Speculative (−3pts)
  55–64%    → Speculative (−3pts)
  50–54%    → Speculative if 2+ of (roe≥0.08, roic≥0.08, eps_next_5y≥0.08) else Dangerous (−8pts)
  <50%      → Dangerous (−8pts)
Moat override: if moat≥7 AND (profitability≥7 OR fs≥7) AND pct≥55 → Safe.
final_score_pct = max(0, final_pct − penalty)

quality_narrative.classification_reason: cbdMD, Inc. carries a Dangerous rating because it shows significant weaknesses in profitability and growth. Revenue and earnings have been inconsistent, making it hard to predict where this business will be in a few years. Dangerous-rated stocks can still recover, but they carry a higher risk of permanent capital loss and should only be held with a clear thesis and strict position sizing.
quality_narrative.investment_narrative: cbdMD, Inc. demonstrates weak fundamental quality and is constrained by profitability. Key risks to watch: a weak near-term growth outlook; inconsistent earnings; balance sheet constraints on growth. The stock trades at a 70% discount to estimated intrinsic value and is warrants caution — careful position sizing and ongoing monitoring are essential.
quality_narrative.quality_conclusion: cbdMD, Inc. demonstrates weak fundamental quality and is constrained by profitability.

_notes.revenue_vs_net_income: This chart shows how much money the company brings in (revenue) versus how much it actually keeps as profit (net income) each year. A healthy company should show both lines growing over time. If revenue grows but net income shrinks, the company is spending more than it is earning — a warning sign worth investigating.
_notes.fcf_vs_debt: Free cash flow is the cash left over after the company has paid for everything it needs to run and grow the business. This chart compares that cash to the company's total debt. A company with rising free cash flow and falling debt is in a strong financial position. If debt is rising faster than cash, the company may be taking on more risk than it can comfortably manage.
